topic.title: Unit Testing
topic.p: An easy-to-use unit test framework. You are able to organize test cases in nested categories, and either run all the way to the end, or tell the IDE to stop at the first failed test assertion.

Calling UnitTest::RunAndDisposeTests to start a unit test CLI application and the following CLI arguments are supported by default:
/D: Debug mode, it will crash at the first error.
/R: Release mode, it will print all crashed tests with their failures.
/C: LLM mode, it will execute until the first crashed test with its failure printed and exit.
/F:FileName.cpp: Only run test cases defined in this file. Multiple instances are allowed at the same time to execute a specified group of files. Wildcards are supported, e.g., /F:Test_My*.cpp. /F only filters test files by file names, file path is not considered.
/D, /R and /C are exclusive. If none of them are offered, on Windows /D is activated if a debugger is attached to the process from the beginning.

topic.title: Mocking DateTime
topic.p: vl::InjectDateTimeImpl could be used to control the implementation of vl::DateTime. This function stack all injections, each injection could access the previous implementation by implementing vl::feature_injection::IFeatureImpl
vl::EjectDateTimeImpl could be used to revert vl::InjectDateTimeImpl. Passing nullptr to cancel all vl::InjectDateTimeImpl calls.

topic.title: Mocking Locale
topic.p: vl::InjectLocaleImpl could be used to control the implementation of vl::Locale. This function stack all injections, each injection could access the previous implementation by implementing vl::feature_injection::IFeatureImpl
vl::EjectLocaleImpl could be used to revert vl::InjectLocaleImpl. Passing nullptr to cancel all vl::InjectLocaleImpl calls.

topic.title: Mocking File, Folder and FileStream
topic.p: vl::filesystem::InjectFileSystemImpl could be used to control the implementation of vl::filesystem::File, vl::filesystem::Folder, and vl::stream::FileStream. This function stack all injections, each injection could access the previous implementation by implementing vl::feature_injection::IFeatureImpl

IFileSystemImpl::GetFileStreamImpl controls how FileStream access a file from a given file path. If virtual files are added to the file system, this function should take care of them. This function can redirect the call to the previous implementation for files that are not added by the current implementation.
vl::filesystem::EjectFileSystemImpl could be used to revert vl::filesystem::InjectFileSystemImpl. Passing nullptr to cancel all vl::filesystem::InjectFileSystemImpl calls.

topic.title: Ordinary Testing
topic.p: The unit test framework is a CLI application. You can do any assertion you want in the TEST_FILE -> {TEST_CATEGORY}* -> TEST_CASE structure.

topic.title: Testing GacUI Application
topic.p: There are some additional features for testing a GacUI application.
You can simulate user interaction, including keyboard, mouse, typing, etc.
You can access controls for assertion even if they are not exposed in public members.

You can log the whole UI into multiple JSON files. The file organization is handled by the unit test framework, and you are able to run a snapshot viewer to render these snapshots for inspection. The snapshot viewer is offered along with the source code as a window class, you need to write a simple GuiMain to run the window offering the snapshot folder path.
